findings.evidence: Existing hits: `TauCeti/Analysis/PDE/CoerciveEnergy.lean:126` defines `min_coercivityConstant_mul_norm_sq_le_energyIntegrand_self`, `:144` defines `isCoercive_energyIntegrand_of_bounds`, and `TauCeti/Analysis/PDE/UniformEllipticity.lean:317` defines `lower_bound_toQuadraticForm'_add`; the new wrappers at `CoercivePerturbation.lean:70`, `:100`, `:113`, `:129`, `:145` only package those with the perturbation assumptions.
findings.fix: Remove these parallel wrappers or replace callers with the existing raw-bound lemmas using `lower_bound_toQuadraticForm'_add hA hB` and `hc.trans (le_add_of_nonneg_right hd)`; keep only the genuinely new diagonal monotonicity/arbitrary-`IsCoercive` lemma if needed.
findings.issue: The perturbed explicit/coercivity wrappers at lines 70, 100, 113, 129, and 145 are special cases of the existing `min_coercivityConstant_mul_norm_sq_le_energyIntegrand_self` / `isCoercive_energyIntegrand_of_bounds` APIs applied to `A + B` and `c₀ + d`.

summary: Several new perturbed coercivity wrappers duplicate the existing raw-bound coercivity API after a one-line lower-bound rewrite, creating parallel entry points for the same estimate.
